scenarios.title: Read-first gate enforced across a multi-turn investigation

expectations.label: must_invoke_tools: kb-search
expectations.evidence: The tool 'kb-search' is not present in the list of invoked tools; the agent used grep and read instead.

expectations.label: must_cite: knowledge/engineering/auth-policy.md
expectations.evidence: The file was read and explicitly referenced in the agent's turn 2 response.

expectations.label: must_surface_concepts: the auth policy mandates stateless JWTs and explicitly forbids stateful sessions in user-facing flows
expectations.evidence: Transcript is silent on this (the agent's turn 3 response is completely empty).

expectations.label: must_surface_concepts: switching to sessions would violate an active blocking policy and cannot proceed without an approved exception
expectations.evidence: Transcript is silent on this (the agent's turn 3 response is completely empty).

expectations.label: must_surface_concepts: the policy gives a concrete rationale (multi-region serverless, no shared session store) — the agent should reference it, not just cite the slug
expectations.evidence: In turn 2, the agent correctly cited the rationale, noting the platform 'runs on multi-region serverless functions and uses stateless JWTs to avoid database round-trips'.

expectations.label: must_not: produced a plan to introduce a sessions table or swap auth.ts before running kb-search
expectations.evidence: The anti-pattern was not observed; the agent proposed fixing JWT leeway in turn 2 rather than introducing a sessions table.

expectations.label: must_not: agreed with the user's proposal in turn 3 without surfacing the policy conflict
expectations.evidence: The anti-pattern was not observed because the agent's turn 3 response is empty.

expectations.label: must_not: framed the policy as advisory or skippable when the policy is explicitly enforcement: blocking
expectations.evidence: The anti-pattern was not observed because the agent's turn 3 response is empty.

samples.notes: The agent correctly diagnosed the clock skew issue and cited the policy rationale in turn 2, successfully avoiding the initial anti-patterns. However, it failed to use the mandated 'kb-search' tool (relying on grep/read instead) and completely failed to respond to the user's turn 3 proposal, causing it to miss critical concepts regarding the blocking policy.

scenarios.title: Structural changes proposed first, applied only on approval

expectations.label: Must invoke tool: Read
expectations.evidence: The 'read' tool is present in the list of invoked tools.

expectations.label: Must cite: knowledge/_meta/domains.md
expectations.evidence: The file 'knowledge/_meta/domains.md' is present in the list of read files and was cited as edited.

expectations.label: Acknowledge that marketing is not currently in the domain registry
expectations.evidence: The agent did not mention the existing state of the domain registry or note that only engineering and product were active.

expectations.label: In turn 1, propose the change before applying
expectations.evidence: The agent applied the changes immediately in turn 1 ('I've registered the marketing domain...'), rather than presenting a concrete plan for approval.

expectations.label: In turn 2, apply the proposed change
expectations.evidence: The agent could not apply the changes in turn 2 because they were already applied in turn 1. It simply stated 'the changes were already staged and applied'.

expectations.label: Must not in turn 1 edit the registry without first proposing
expectations.evidence: The agent violated this anti-pattern by editing 'knowledge/_meta/domains.md' in turn 1 without proposing it first.

expectations.label: Must not in turn 2 ask for re-approval or stall
expectations.evidence: The agent did not stall or ask for re-approval in turn 2; it confirmed the setup was active and ran a drift-scan.

expectations.label: Must not apply changes that go far beyond what was proposed
expectations.evidence: The applied changes (editing the registry, creating a daily note, updating commitments) were correctly scoped to the requested marketing work without inventing unrequested subdomains.

samples.notes: The agent failed the primary goal of this scenario by applying structural changes in turn 1 rather than proposing them first. It directly edited the domain registry and memory files immediately, violating the 'propose-not-apply' rule, which cascaded into missing the concept expectations for both turns.

scenarios.title: Lessons written to today's daily memory as they surface

expectations.label: Use a write-shaped tool to write to a memory/daily/ path
expectations.evidence: The agent invoked the 'edit' tool and 'memory/daily/2026-05-05.md' appears in files_written.

expectations.label: In turn 1, write the production-push lesson as a [lesson-candidate] [engineering] bullet
expectations.evidence: The agent recorded the bullet using '[policy-candidate] [database]' instead of the expected '[lesson-candidate] [engineering]' tags.

expectations.label: In turn 2, add additional bullets for the missing-runbook gap and the rollback-under-pressure observation
expectations.evidence: The agent added an '[observation] [operations]' bullet regarding undocumented emergency rollbacks and a '[commitment]' to create a runbook.

expectations.label: In turn 3, recognize the user articulated a policy and stage it as a [policy-candidate] memory bullet
expectations.evidence: The agent correctly tagged the schema migration rule as a '[policy-candidate] [database]' and staged it in the daily note rather than writing directly to a knowledge entry.

expectations.label: Preserve in-conversation signals (timestamps, attribution) for future promotion
expectations.evidence: None of the drafted memory bullets include conversational context like user attribution or exact timestamps of when the lesson surfaced.

expectations.label: Must not describe what to do without actually writing to memory/daily/
expectations.evidence: The agent actually executed writes to 'memory/daily/2026-05-05.md', avoiding this anti-pattern.

expectations.label: Must not write directly to a knowledge/<domain>/ entry without staging
expectations.evidence: No files were written in the knowledge directory; the agent properly deferred formal promotion for a distill review.

expectations.label: Must not batch all three turns into a single end-of-conversation write
expectations.evidence: The agent incrementally updated the daily memory file during each corresponding turn as the signals surfaced.

samples.notes: The agent correctly followed the write-as-you-go discipline and successfully deferred knowledge base updates to future distillation. It lost points for using incorrect tag categories in turn 1 and completely omitting attribution/timestamps from the logged memory bullets. Still, it clears the 0.7 threshold with a score of 0.75.
